headline: Oil Prices Dive As Trump Say Iran 'Excursion' May Soon Be Over; S&P 500 Rallies
summary: Oil prices, after jumping to nearly $120 a barrel, plunged below $90 on Monday amid news that G7 countries may jointly release of petroleum reserves and on word from President Trump that the war may soon be over.  CBS News reporter Weijia Jiang said Trump told her in an interview that the U.S. is "very far" ahead of his initial estimate of a four-week conflict and that the war could be over "soon."  G7 nations are set to meet Tuesday and might announce a release of reserves, buying some time for U.S. and Israeli combat operations to attempt to extinguish Iran's threat to Strait of Hormuz traffic and regional oil infrastructure.

headline: Oil Prices Back Below $100 Amid Iran 'Excursion,' Despite This Surprise
summary: Oil prices, after jumping to nearly $120 a barrel, came down amid news that G7 countries might announce a joint release of petroleum reserves on Monday morning.  The surprise was that the G7 stopped short of making such a move, saying only that they were prepared to act.  For now, markets largely align with President Donald Trump's comment to reporters on Saturday that the war was a "short excursion," suggesting that the oil price surge was temporary.

headline: Here Are Monday’s Top Wall Street Analyst Research Calls: Brown-Forman, Cava Group, Dow, GE Vernova, Global Payments, Netflix, Shake Shack,  Starbucks, Verizon, and More
summary: Pre-Market Stock Futures: Futures are trading lower once again on Monday, following a horrible week on Wall Street. With all of Wall Street looking for a solid non-farm payrolls report of 65,000 jobs added, it came in at a very disappointing 92,000 jobs lost, and the unemployment rate bumped up to 4.4%.
